personal.name: Mohssine Echlaihi
personal.title: Software Engineering Student | Java Backend Developer
personal.description: Fourth-year Computer Engineering student at ENSA Al Hoceima (ENSAH), focused on Java backend development and distributed systems. Hands-on experience designing Spring Boot microservices, REST APIs, and full-stack applications. Fast learner, strong problem-solver, and active open-source / Linux user. Seeking a PFA internship (2-3 months) to contribute to real-world backend or full-stack projects.
personal.headline: Building scalable backend systems & distributed architectures

responsibilities: Designed and built a complete license management platform end-to-end: requirements analysis, system design, implementation, and deployment
Developed backend with Laravel (MVC), implemented authentication and core business logic
Built frontend with Blade, HTML, and CSS
Designed and optimized the PostgreSQL schema
Containerized the app with Docker and managed releases via GitLab

projects.name: Student Enrollment System - Microservices Architecture
projects.description: A distributed system for managing students, courses, and enrollments using microservices architecture
features: Independent Spring Boot services (Student, Course, Enrollment) communicating via WebClient behind an API Gateway
Database-per-service design (MySQL) for loose coupling and scalability
Business rules implementation including capacity limits and cancellation policies

projects.name: University Academic Management Platform
projects.description: Multi-role academic platform with granular role-based access control
features: Multi-role system (admin, professor, coordinator, department head) with granular role-based access control
Custom Laravel middleware for route protection and role/permission verification
Normalized relational schema (MySQL) with Eloquent ORM relationships (one-to-many, many-to-many)
Core modules for courses, scheduling, and grade management
Laravel's validation layer and form requests for data integrity across all CRUD operations
